item.title: Brazil Inflation Surges Past Forecasts on Iran War Energy Shock
item.description: Brazil’s inflation accelerated more than expected in March as the Iran war’s energy shock ripples through Latin America’s largest economy.

item.title: US Stocks’ Seven-Day Rally Stalls Ahead of CPI, US-Iran Talks
item.description: US equity futures stalled after a seven-day rally as investors weighed whether a fragile truce between Washington and Tehran can hold, and oil headed for its biggest weekly loss in nine months.

item.title: GQG’s Kersmanc Says Early Big Tech Exit Is Finally Paying Off
item.description: Brian Kersmanc at GQG Partners LLC says a decision to empty his funds of tech companies more than a year ago is finally starting to pay off.

item.title: US Premarket Movers: CoreWeave, Docusign, Lumentum, Organon
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures are little changed as of 7:50 a.m. in New York. Its seven-day winning streak, the longest run for the benchmark since October, looks set to be tested as traders prepare for CPI data.

item.title: Demand for Dollars in Currency Swaps Ebbs Alongside Global Risks
item.description: By at least one metric in the $9.5 trillion foreign-exchange markets, demand for the dollar is ebbing amid the tenuous ceasefire between the US and Iran.

item.title: Anthropic Will Rent CoreWeave AI Capacity to Power Claude
item.description: Anthropic PBC agreed to rent data center capacity from CoreWeave Inc. as part of efforts to handle increasing demand for its artificial intelligence services.

item.title: US CPI Data Offer First Look at Iran War’s Inflation Impact
item.description: Shoppers in the SoHo neighborhood of New York. Photographer: Michael Nagle/Bloomberg

item.title: Finance Titans Bet Mideast Resilience Will Outweigh War Fallout
item.description: Late last month, Blackstone Inc. announced the first inbound Gulf private equity deal since Iran started attacking Middle Eastern hubs, while Citigroup Inc.’s top boss fired off a 600-word memo underlining the bank’s enthusiasm for its business in the region.

item.title: Binance Offers Temporary Relocation to UAE-Based Employees
item.description: Binance Holdings Ltd. said it is offering temporary relocation to employees based in the United Arab Emirates, as the Iran conflict continues to impact countries across the Middle East.

item.title: Nuclear Power Takes Another Tiny Step Forward in the US
item.description: New Jersey’s governor removes a moratorium on new reactors.

item.title: Ares Plans Smaller Private Credit Fund After Record Predecessor
item.description: Ares Management Corp. is planning a significantly smaller flagship US direct lending fund than its previous record-breaking vehicle of $33.6 billion to speed up deployment of capital, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Nvidia’s Huang Urges Companies to Put AI Breakthroughs Before Profit
item.description: Leading booster for AI says companies are too focused on an immediate payoff

item.title: Global Rate Outlook Tilts Higher After Trump War Shock
item.description: Trump’s second big shock to the world economy since he returned to the White House looks increasingly likely to force central banks into a new round of tightening.

item.title: UnitedHealth’s Last Bear Stands Pat After Medicare Rate Hike
item.description: UnitedHealth Group Inc.’s last remaining bear is standing by his call even as the stock heads toward its best week in seven months after a surprise hike in Medicare rates.

item.title: Universal Music Is Trading at Just Two Thirds of Ackman’s Offer
item.description: Three days after Bill Ackman pitched a deal for Universal Music Group NV that he estimates values the record label giant at €56 billion ($66 billion), the Dutch company’s stock remains steadfastly below the offer price.

item.title: Retail Stock Traders Turn Into Sellers as Rally Spurs Skepticism
item.description: A bout of equities volatility fueled by surging energy prices amid the Iran war is eroding confidence among some of the market’s most reliable bulls: retail investors.

item.title: Glencore Chrome Venture Provisionally OKs Cheaper Power Deal
item.description: Glencore Plc’s ferrochrome operation in South Africa initially accepted an offer for cheaper electricity as the company and the state negotiate the restart of the firm’s smelters.

item.title: Bank Earnings Offer a Reset After Stocks’ Worst Quarter in Years
item.description: With war raging in the Middle East and private credit fears shaking markets, shares of Wall Street’s biggest banks are off to their worst start to a year since the regional banking crisis. But with the stocks now trading at relatively cheap valuations, strong earnings next week could spark a rally.

item.title: Hungary Assets Rally Into Sunday’s Election on Orban-Exit Bets
item.description: Hungary’s election rally has pushed the country’s bonds and currency to the brink of multi-year highs ahead of Sunday’s ballot, which investors anticipate will end Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s 16-year rule.

item.title: BofA’s Hartnett Sees the Surge in Commodities Lasting for Years
item.description: Investors should flock into commodities for the next few years as they benefit from global geopolitical and macroeconomic turmoil, according to Bank of America’s Michael Hartnett.

item.title: Gold May See Long-Term Rebound Despite War Setbacks, Banks Say
item.description: Gold is likely to rebound in the long-term even as the Middle East war upended the market, according to banks including ANZ Banking Group Ltd. and Goldman Sachs Group Inc.

item.title: Investors Emboldened as Iran Ceasefire Appears to Hold
item.description: US stocks have climbed for seven straight days.

item.title: Rubio to Visit India in May as Ties With New Delhi Improve
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio is set to visit India in May as New Delhi and Washington seek to reset ties strained by US tariff policies.

item.title: Japan Lowers View of China Relations Amid Tension Over Taiwan
item.description: Japan slightly downgraded its assessment of relations with China in its annual foreign policy report released Friday, as ties between the two Asian giants remain strained over views on Taiwan.

item.title: UBS Wins Dismissal of Credit Suisse Money-Laundering Charges Tied to Mozambique Tuna Bond Scandal
item.description: UBS Group AG won dismissal of a money-laundering case it inherited from its troubled former rival Credit Suisse linked to the so-called Mozambique tuna-bond scandal.

item.title: SAP Extends Chief People Officer Contract After Bonus Complaints
item.description: SAP SE has extended the contract of its Chief People Officer Gina Vargiu-Breuer months after internal criticism of her department over what many employees described as a flawed bonus system.

item.title: China Revamps ChiNext Rules to Lure More Startups to Market
item.description: China unveiled new rules for the Shenzhen stock exchange’s tech-focused ChiNext board, seeking to broaden listing options and make the market more attractive to fast‑growing companies.

item.title: Jack Ma’s Yunfeng, Hillhouse Are Said to Plan Investing in Victory Giant’s $2 Billion Listing
item.description: Chinese billionaire Jack Ma’s Yunfeng Capital Co. and alternative-asset manager Hillhouse Investment are among key investors planning to buy shares in the Hong Kong listing of Victory Giant Technology Huizhou Co., according to people familiar with the matter, in what could be among the city’s largest maiden share sales this year.

item.title: UK Signs Drone Interceptor Deal With Cambridge Aerospace
item.description: Britain has signed a “multimillion-pound” contract with defense startup Cambridge Aerospace to supply interceptors to counter cheaper missiles like those that Iran has fired around the Persian Gulf in recent weeks.

item.title: Oil to Test Wartime Highs If Hormuz Standstill Drags, JPM Warns
item.description: Oil looks set to test levels seen at the height of the war in the Middle East if cargoes shipped through the Strait of Hormuz take until July to fully recover, according to JPMorgan Chase & Co.
